title: artemisia campestris subsp. lempergii
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. lempergii, also known as Lemperg's mugwort, is a subspecies of the mugwort plant, a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Artemisia campestris subsp. lempergii include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and infusions, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
